title: known-bad
description: The ERROR-PATH contract lock (§8: 'a known-bad document asserting diagnostics extraction'). \usepackage{nosuchpackagexyz} requests a package that exists in NO tier: it is not a provided-package name and nosuchpackagexyz.sty is not a file in any tier's ls-R. So the §5.4(a) scan resolves it to nothing; pass 1 fails 'File `nosuchpackagexyz.sty' not found'; the §5.4(b) missing-file retry makes exactly ONE bounded mount attempt (the sound over-approximation with a single on-demand tier — see bundle-resolution.ts), academic mounts, the re-run STILL cannot find the file, and the job fails cleanly. This is BOUNDED, not spurious: bundlesLoaded=['core','academic'] with no loop, ok:false, exitCode 1, and NO output PDF ('Fatal error occurred, no output PDF file produced!'). The runner asserts the parsed diagnostic shape — severity/message/file/line — with the error attributed to main.tex line 3 (\begin{document}, the emergency-stop point). No resolution is pinned because the retry mounted but did NOT resolve (the file is genuinely absent).
